task_language: Pick the akita black bowl next to the ramekin and place it on the plate
perturbation_semantics: Two identical akita_black_bowls in scene; the target is disambiguated by spatial relation ('next to the ramekin'), not by name index.
strategy_notes: Localized via world_hi_00.npy clustering (z>0.92 elevated mask, scipy.ndimage.label). Found 3 round-object clusters on the robot-LEFT side: comp3 at world (-0.21, +0.34, 0.94), comp4 at world (-0.21, +0.20, 0.94), comp5 at world (+0.03, +0.215, 0.94). Plate at world (+0.115, -0.045, 0.91), cookies box at (+0.05, -0.02). The largest of the three round objects (comp5, the porcelain dish with crinkled rim in middle-right of image) is the ramekin. The two akita black bowls are the back-area dishes comp3 and comp4. Distance from each bowl to the ramekin: comp4-ramekin = sqrt(0.24^2+0.01^2) = 0.24m, comp3-ramekin = sqrt(0.24^2+0.13^2) = 0.27m. So comp4 ((-0.21, +0.20)) is 'next to the ramekin' (Δy ~ 0.01m vs Δy ~ 0.13m for comp3). Matched seed-0 reference recipe coordinates within 2cm; reused the winning sequence: pre-position above target -> pi0_pick 'pick up the akita black bowl' (max_chunks=22, lift=0.06, grip=0.06) -> set_gripper +1 firm hold -> two-waypoint carry at z=1.13 to clear obstacles -> descend to z=1.04 over plate with eef_y_target = plate_y - 0.03 (bowl-eef yaw-flipped offset, per reference) -> release. SOLVED in 8 commands first try.

pick_result.name: pick
pick_result.instruction: pick up the akita black bowl
